brief_justification: Agent chose (B) while ground truth is (A), but the reasoning approach analyzing stock-vs-flow was internally sound, earning partial credit; Agent traces second and third-order effects across four pollutant types with mechanism-level analysis; Agent explicitly names the reasoning trap to avoid ('avoid leaping from observation to conclusion') showing process awareness but no mid-course correction; Agent verifies by cross-checking each pollutant's measured result against predicted result given degradation properties and 80% reduction; Agent distinguishes emission rate from river concentration stock but presents the stock-flow distinction with full confidence and no stated uncertainty about boundary conditions; Agent goes directly to (B) without evaluating why (A), (C), or (D) might be credible alternatives or why they fail; Every step from pollutant property to expected measurement to agency error is explicitly derived and reproducible by a third party; Agent recognizes prior environmental modification (accumulated heavy metals as a pre-existing trace) consistent with avoiding the passive-substrate assumption, but does not explicitly name the negative gate; Agent avoids observational causation inference and correlation-to-causation leap (its own suppression terms) and also avoids treating the river as unchanged, covering most suppressed modes; Reasoning proceeds linearly through pollutant types rather than following the S1→N→S2→ACC→M1 branching/gate/loop structure of the provided topology.
